verses.text: Solomon tried pleasure and wine, keeping his wisdom, to see what was good for men. He tested mirth as an experiment. But laughter and drink, pursued for meaning, prove only madness and vanity.
Solomon tried pleasure and wine, keeping his wisdom, to see what was good for men. He tested mirth as an experiment. But laughter and drink, pursued for meaning, prove only madness and vanity.
Solomon tried pleasure and wine, keeping his wisdom, to see what was good for men. He tested mirth as an experiment. But laughter and drink, pursued for meaning, prove only madness and vanity.
He built houses, planted vineyards and gardens, gathered servants, silver, gold, and every delight. No king ever had more. Yet the grandest works and riches the world offers cannot fill the soul.

He built houses, planted vineyards and gardens, gathered servants, silver, gold, and every delight. No king ever had more. Yet the grandest works and riches the world offers cannot fill the soul.

Greater than all before him, he withheld no desire from his eyes, then looked on all his works: 'all was vanity and vexation of spirit.' The harvest of indulgence was emptiness. Having everything, he found no profit under the sun.

Greater than all before him, he withheld no desire from his eyes, then looked on all his works: 'all was vanity and vexation of spirit.' The harvest of indulgence was emptiness. Having everything, he found no profit under the sun.

Greater than all before him, he withheld no desire from his eyes, then looked on all his works: 'all was vanity and vexation of spirit.' The harvest of indulgence was emptiness. Having everything, he found no profit under the sun.

Turning to compare wisdom and folly, he saw the wise man's eyes in his head while the fool walks in darkness, 'yet one event happeneth to them all.' Death levels both. Even wisdom's advantage is shadowed by the grave that takes wise and foolish alike.

Turning to compare wisdom and folly, he saw the wise man's eyes in his head while the fool walks in darkness, 'yet one event happeneth to them all.' Death levels both. Even wisdom's advantage is shadowed by the grave that takes wise and foolish alike.

Turning to compare wisdom and folly, he saw the wise man's eyes in his head while the fool walks in darkness, 'yet one event happeneth to them all.' Death levels both. Even wisdom's advantage is shadowed by the grave that takes wise and foolish alike.
Since the wise dies as the fool, and both are soon forgotten, Solomon 'hated life.' The common end embittered him. When life is measured only by what stays under the sun, despair is its conclusion.

Since the wise dies as the fool, and both are soon forgotten, Solomon 'hated life.' The common end embittered him. When life is measured only by what stays under the sun, despair is its conclusion.

He hated his labour, since he must leave its fruit to a successor who may be wise or foolish. The lifetime's gain passes to another's hand. To toil and not keep is one of the bitter vanities of earthly striving.

He hated his labour, since he must leave its fruit to a successor who may be wise or foolish. The lifetime's gain passes to another's hand. To toil and not keep is one of the bitter vanities of earthly striving.
What does a man gain but sorrowful days and a heart that rests not even at night? Toil without God yields only grief. The anxious round of labour cannot give the soul its rest.
What does a man gain but sorrowful days and a heart that rests not even at night? Toil without God yields only grief. The anxious round of labour cannot give the soul its rest.

Yet there is good: to eat, drink, and enjoy one's labour as 'from the hand of God,' who gives wisdom and joy to the good. Even here a ray breaks through. The simple gifts of life, received from God, are to be enjoyed with thanks.
